# Transcrição: 02  The USP Factory

## [00:00 - 03:01]

What is a USP? A USP is simply a unique selling position or a unique selling point. This is really the big, big picture behind what it is that you're selling. And it's far, far more than a mission statement. This is actually not a mission statement. Now, you can use your USP as a mission statement if you like, but USP is very succinct. It's very succinct. And there's a formula that I'm going to give you to write one. And this is going to lay the foundation for all of your marketing and communication to your buyers and potential buyers. And as we go along, as you go along today through this course, you're going to be freaked out by how important this one element is. And when you see how it plugs into the VSL formats and the VSL formula, it's going to get real clear how vital this is and how much time you really need to put into this one part. This is the vital importance of knowing what your USP is, your true USP as I call it. Your USP is going to be used in so many stages of the one, two, three formula. And without a properly defined USP, your marketing will miss the target. It's that simple. And you certainly don't want to do that when it comes to any sales page or any video sales letter. Your true USP is best defined as how your buyers view your business. If you cater to your buyer's desires and their understanding of what your business is, you will always profit more in the long run. So let's look at an example of creating a USP. Okay, one of my products is called the Every Other Day Diet. Perhaps you've heard of it. And my top three problems that this product solves for my readers are, well, the first one's obvious. It helps people lose weight. The second one is it does not restrict dieters from their favorite foods. And the third one is it provides them a lifestyle eating system. Now when I wrote down, these are the three problems that my book solves. It solves more than that, but these are the top three. What I came up with is the number one problem that really makes this stand up away from the competition is that problem number two, this does not restrict dieters from their favorite foods. That's a pretty powerful claim. So I'm going to use that as the foundational model, what I call the primary benefit of my USP. I'm going to show you how this looks. First of all, let me show you how it shouldn't look. This is a crude USP, an eating plan that allows you to eat your favorite foods. Okay, that's again, that was my number one. My number one problem solver was that, but putting that as a USP will simply not fly. That is not a powerful USP. It's getting there, but you can do much better. And here is how you can do much better. It's going to require three elements to perfect. You need to write this down. You will include a power phrase. We'll go over that here in just a second. The prime benefit and what I call an enabler phrase. What does this enable? Your client, your visitor, whoever it is that you are servicing to do. And it's almost always a verb. What does this enable someone to do? Let me give you what the actual USP is for my product.

## [03:01 - 05:32]

The only lifestyle eating plan that allows you to eat all your favorite foods and still lose weight. Now here's what confuses people, and I'm going to make it so clear, hopefully it will unconfuse everybody. The power phrase is the only. Very exclusive there, power phrase. The benefit phrase. What's the biggest benefit? Again, I chose eat your favorite foods. The benefit phrase would be that allows you to eat your favorite foods. I put the word allows in there because it's a very powerful word. But notice what the enabler phrase is. And lose weight, and still lose weight in this case. Think about that for a moment. A lot of people may mistake that enabler phrase for your benefit. What's the benefit? Well, it allows you to lose weight. No, the benefit is the most powerful part of your USP. In my case, you get to eat your favorite foods. It enables you to lose weight. So that is very, very key to understand. What your USP allows your user to do. What does it enable them to do better? What does it enable them to accomplish? The benefits just fall into the middle there. So now you have a very solid USP. Now here's some assignments I want to give you. Please write this down. Number one, I want you to take your number one problem solver and make this your prime benefit. What I mean by prime benefit is exactly what we just covered. One of them is what this enables your user client to do. Make sense? Three include a power phrase. A power phrase is just how you're going to introduce this entire thing to the world. What is it going to say? How is it going to say it? So is it the only, is it something that transforms? Is it something that enables? You can use any of those kind of power phrases that you want. I want you to really focus right now on what it is that your business, your product, your service, what it is that it does best in the most unique thing that it does for your clients because that is going to be the focal point for your video sales letter and for that matter, all of your marketing communications.
